title: American Communities 1776-2026
description: Swarthmore College Libraries marks "America250" with a special exhibition in McCabe Library. Drawing on art and artifacts from Special Collections, the exhibition explores how family, land, and nation have shaped the idea of “America,” and invites visitors to consider how they see this country and its history today.

title: Pick up a Study Abroad Graduation Cord!
description:
Attention graduating seniors who studied abroad for a semester or academic year!

Pick-up an honor cord to display your study abroad achievements during graduation. Cords are available for pickup this week.


  • Wednesday (5/13): 1:00-4:00pm, Cunningham House
  • Thursday (5/14): 12:00-2:00pm, DCC (Table past entrance)
  • Friday (5/15): 9:00am-11:00am, Cunningham House

Contact geo@swarthmore.edu with any questions. Congratulations, Class of 2026!

title: Philadelphia Connection Woodmere Art Museum Tour
description:
Woodmere Art Museum Tour

Charles Knox Smith Hall
9201 Germantown Avenue
Philadelphia

$15 per person
RSVP Today

This landmark exhibition celebrates the fifty-year career of former Swarthmore professor Syd Carpenter. Together, we will explore her evocative ceramic sculptures that weave together African American history, ancestral lands, and the beauty of the natural world. It is a wonderful opportunity to engage with profound art while reconnecting with your local alumni community. Then join us for an optional group lunch after the tour to continue our conversations. Lunch venue details will be shared very soon.

Warmly,
The Philadelphia Connection Committee

title: 2026 CCC Spring Concert
description:
Join our 110 Chester children for their spring concerts featuring classical and choral works, followed by The Beatles' greatest hits, including "Hey Jude," "Help!," Lucy in the Sky With Diamonds," "Let It Be," and more.

These will be the final performances of CCC Founder, Executive & Artistic Director John Alston, before his retirement in August 2026.

"Who doesn't want to finish a musical career with The Beatles? They gave us some of the world's most compelling melodies," said Alston. "I can barely contain myself when our children sing the 'Hey Jude' hook."

Guests are invited to post-concert receptions in the Lang Performing Arts Center (LPAC), which is adjacent to the Lang Concert Hall.

Please note: due to extraordinary interest in John Alston’s final concerts before his retirement, we’ve added a third show. We ask guests to attend one performance, so more people can share in this special moment.

Overflow seating will be available next door in the Lang Performing Arts Center (LPAC), where guests can watch the performance livestream.
location: Lang Performing Arts Center Lobby (LPAC), Lang Music Building Room 214-Presser Room, Lang Music Concert Hall, Lang Music Building Upper Lobby, Lang Music Building Lower Lobby

title: The Garnet Choir is touring the United Kingdom this May
description:
The Garnet Choir is touring the United Kingdom this May. We invite our UK alumni to attend any of the following performances to hear the ensemble’s current repertoire.

The students would love to see you in the audience as they share their work with international listeners.

Performance Schedule:

Saturday, May 16 | 7:30 p.m. Performing with the Reading Bach Choir at St. James's Church, Sussex Gardens, London (W2 3UD). Purchase tickets here for £15.00. A meet-and-greet will follow the performance, with choir members and alumni continuing the evening at The Swan for those who would like to join.

Tuesday, May 19 | 5:30 p.m. Evensong with Selwyn College Choir at Selwyn College Chapel, Cambridge

Wednesday, May 20 | 5:30 p.m. Evensong at Ely Cathedral, Ely

Thursday, May 21 | 1:00 p.m. Lunchtime Concert at Ely Cathedral, Ely

There is no formal registration or fee required for these events, except for the St. James's Church performance — simply arrive and enjoy the music. Guests are more than welcome to attend, so please feel free to bring family and friends along.

We hope you can attend and enjoy a performance during the Garnet Choir’s UK tour!

Warmly,
Swarthmore College Alumni

title: Pilates with Cathy Raymond
description:
Pilates is a progressive system of exercises designed to stretch, strengthen, and balance both mind and body. Benefits include increased core strength, better posture and balance skills, more physical and mental energy, and improved sports performance.

A recording of each class is available on YouTube.

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: RIP "Final_v2_ACTUAL_FINAL": Organizing Your Research Like a Pro
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

Stop searching for "Final_v2_ACTUAL_FINAL" and start researching! This workshop covers the "hidden curriculum" of digital research—from professional file naming and Google Drive organization to advanced collaboration techniques. We’ll start with the basics of digital hygiene and finish with an introduction to the College’s High-Performance Computing (HPC) resources for those ready to scale up their data. 

Presented by Jen Moore, Academic Technologist for the Arts & Humanities, ITS.

title: Hatha Yoga with Stacy Bomento
description: This Hatha yoga class will focus on breathing and mindful movement, helping to increase strength and flexibility. Offering modifications for various postures, this class is accessible to all levels of practice. Each practice will include poses, breathing exercises, and a restorative and calming finish, followed by guided meditation. **There will be no class on July 21**
location: Matchbox Wellness Lounge Upper Level, Matchbox Tarble Commons Upper Level

title: Garnet Day
description:
Join our annual tradition of celebrating Swarthmore seniors during May’s Garnet Day! Please note that, since Thursday is the first day of exams, Garnet Day will be on Wednesday this month.

Garnet Day Celebration of the Class of 2026

Wednesday, May 6

4-6 p.m.

Clothier Hall

There will be carnival games, music, and an array of snacks, including treats from Weckerly’s, Lil’ Pop Shop, and the Dia Dolce cupcake truck. Vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free food options will be available. Everyone’s favorite feathered friend, Phineas, will also make an appearance! 

We hope you can stop by to congratulate our seniors on their remarkable accomplishments, and wish them a wonderful future ahead. As always, remember to wear your Swarthmore gear to show your spirit!

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: The Honest Scholar: Best Practices in the Ethical Conduct of Research
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

Students will learn the basic rules for ethical conduct of research, and the three (3) things that constitute research misconduct. Think Plagiarism! We'll consider responsible conduct of research (RCR) principles and how they apply to your scholarly activities, and research of all kinds, whether in the lab, the archive, or the field.

Presented by Jena Fioravanti, Director of the Office of Research Integrity and Engagement.

title: Peter van de Kamp Observatory Open House
description:
The Physics and Astronomy department hosts a telescope open house on the second Tuesday of each month at the Peter van de Kamp Observatory on the roof of the Science Center. Directions are available at www.swarthmore.edu/peter-van-de-kamp-observatory/directions.

The open house hours are 8:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. during standard time and 9:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. during daylight savings time.

If the weather is bad, the open house will be cancelled and an announcement will be posted on the Physics and Astronomy department website and the College calendar. Check https://www.swarthmore.edu/physics-astronomy on the day of the open house if the weather is questionable

Open to the public.
location: Science Center Peter Van de Kamp Observatory, Science Center Observatory Computer Room

title: Pilates with Cathy Raymond
description:
Pilates is a progressive system of exercises designed to stretch, strengthen, and balance both mind and body. Benefits include increased core strength, better posture and balance skills, more physical and mental energy, and improved sports performance.

A recording of each class is available on YouTube.

title: American Communities 1776-2026
description: Swarthmore College Libraries marks "America250" with a special exhibition in McCabe Library. Drawing on art and artifacts from Special Collections, the exhibition explores how family, land, and nation have shaped the idea of “America,” and invites visitors to consider how they see this country and its history today.

title: Hatha Yoga with Stacy Bomento
description: This Hatha yoga class will focus on breathing and mindful movement, helping to increase strength and flexibility. Offering modifications for various postures, this class is accessible to all levels of practice. Each practice will include poses, breathing exercises, and a restorative and calming finish, followed by guided meditation. **There will be no class on July 23**
location: Matchbox Wellness Lounge Upper Level, Matchbox Tarble Commons Upper Level

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: The Honest Scholar: Best Practices in the Ethical Conduct of Research
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

Students will learn the basic rules for ethical conduct of research, and the three (3) things that constitute research misconduct. Think Plagiarism! We'll consider responsible conduct of research (RCR) principles and how they apply to your scholarly activities, and research of all kinds, whether in the lab, the archive, or the field.

Presented by Jena Fioravanti, Director of the Office of Research Integrity and Engagement.

title: Group Circuit Training with Chris
description: Do you struggle to figure out which workout to do at the gym, or do you need some extra motivation? If so, this class is for you. Receive personal attention to help you improve your overall fitness through a total body workout. The class will offer modifications or challenges to suit each participant. Missed a workout? Review past workouts online.
location: Matchbox Lower Level Lewis Reynolds Performance Center

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: Research Ready
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

Learn how library resources like Zotero can support your success this summer. We'll cover practical ways to get organized and build stronger research skills. Whether you're part of a team or working solo, you'll walk away with new tools and strategies to try.

Presented by Simon Elichko, Social Sciences & Data Librarian, and Andrea Baruzzi, STEM Librarian.

title: Staff Summer Reading: Food Writing Meet-Up
description: Love food writing? Find fellow readers of the genre, share recommendations, and borrow library books that speak to your interests! This program is part of Staff Summer Reading, brought to you by Swarthmore College Libraries, PSAC, the Office of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion, and a Be Well mini grant. Light refreshments will be served.

title: Canning & Preservation Basics
description: Discover safe, simple methods for preserving seasonal ingredients

title: 2026 Solstice Yoga & Sound Bath
description: Celebrate the summer solstice with an evening of yoga and a sound bath in the beautiful Scott Outdoor Amphitheater. Presented in partnership with the Park Avenue Community Center.

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: AI Use for Research
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

In this workshop students will learn about the benefits and drawbacks of using artificial intelligence as a research tool, including in depth discussion of the ethical considerations. Students will learn how to access our data-protected, licensed AI tools supported by ITS and Swarthmore Libraries.

Presented by Amanda Licastro, Head of Digital Scholarship and Visiting Associate Professor of English.

description:
Join us for a workshop on the mint family - we'll learn about what's in this family, then make infused honey and a sugar scrub. 

Please sign up here: www.signupgenius.com…

The event will occur in the Wister Center.

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: Communications in a Professional Environment
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

Through this interactive presentation, participants will learn key strategies for professional written and verbal communication, including email etiquette, meeting participation, and clear expectation-setting with supervisors. The session also explores navigating team dynamics, addressing workplace challenges, and engaging in productive feedback conversations. Students will build confidence and comfort in a multitude of scenarios.

Presented by Kelly Dougherty, Assistant Director Employer Relations, Career Services.

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: Balance and Boundaries
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

Learn ways to set and maintain realistic boundaries to better manage your time and support your personal well-being. In this workshop, we will explore strategies to find balance and thrive in academic and professional settings.

Presented by Dean Liz Derickson, Associate Dean of Academic Success; Melissa Mandos, Director of Student Academic Mentors and Fellowships & Prizes Advisor; and Ashley Netanel, Associate Director of Health Promotion & Wellness Initiatives.

title: Crum Woods Walk
description:
Join us for a morning walk through the Crum Woods.  We'll start at the bottom of the Scott Amphitheater and walk approximately one mile to the Matchbox.

Please wear sturdy shoes and bring a water bottle.  This is not a fast walk, but we will be on uneven terrain and walking up and down the hills.

No need to sign up.
